task_completion.description: Verify the 'world_survey' project is open and the 'field_observations' layer is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField interface shows the 'world_survey' project loaded, with the layer panel or active editing mode indicating 'field_observations' is selected.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent determines the coordinates for Rome and Dublin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the feature information panel, search results, or map crosshairs centered on Rome (to find latitude) and Dublin (to find longitude).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent starts creating a new point feature in the correct layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField interface shows the map in digitizing/editing mode with the crosshair visible, specifically for adding a point to the 'field_observations' layer.

task_completion.description: Verify the new feature is placed at the intersection of Rome's latitude and Dublin's longitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map crosshair coordinates or manual coordinate entry fields show approximately Latitude 41.9029 and Longitude -6.2672 (or 41.9° N, 6.2° W).

task_completion.description: Verify the Name or Notes attribute is set to 'Climate Control Site'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The feature attribute form is open and the text 'Climate Control Site' is typed into the Name or Notes text field.

task_completion.description: Verify the new feature is successfully saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The attribute form is confirmed/checked, and the new point is visible on the map at the target location within the 'field_observations' layer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the QField graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the QField app interface (map, menus, attribute forms) rather than a terminal emulator running SQLite commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual mapping application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is navigating the map and using QField's built-in digitizing tools, not just writing the coordinates in a text editor or notes app.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually created the feature through the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the agent manually positioning the crosshair or typing the coordinates and attributes into the QField forms, rather than the point magically appearing without UI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target coordinates for the new feature are Latitude 41.9029 (derived from Rome) and Longitude -6.2672 (derived from Dublin). The new feature must be created in the 'field_observations' layer. The attribute 'Name' or 'Notes' must contain exactly 'Climate Control Site'. The acceptable tolerance for the coordinate placement is 0.5 degrees Euclidean distance.
